name: Two Sum
description:
Find a pair of indices `i` and `j` in an integer array `nums` such that `nums[i] + nums[j]` equals `target`, with the condition that `i != j`.

Each input is guaranteed to have one unique solution. Return the indices as a pair `[i, j]` with the smaller index first.
constraints: *Exactly one solution exists for each testcase.*

name: Contains Duplicates
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices `i` and `j` such as that `nums[i] + nums[j] == target` and `i != j`.

You may assume that *every* input has exactly 1 pair of indices `i` and `j` that satisfy this condition.

Return the answer with the smaller index first.

name: Valid Anagram
description: Determine if string `t` is a permutation of string `s` (an anagram), meaning it contains the exact same characters with the same frequencies, just possibly in a different order. Return `True` if it is, and `False` otherwise.
constraints: `s` and `t` consist of lowercase English letters.

name: Design Dynamic Array (Resizable Array)
description:
Design a resizable array class named `DynamicArray` to manage a list of integers dynamically.

The class should implement the following functionality:

* `DynamicArray(capacity)` initializes the array with a starting capacity of `capacity` (always greater than zero).
* `get(i)` retrieves the value at index `i`.
* `set(i, val)` replaces the value at index `i` with `val`.
* `pushback(val)` appends `val` to the end of the array, automatically doubling the capacity via `resize()` if the array is full.
* `popback()` removes and returns the last element of the array.
* `resize()` doubles the current capacity of the array, copying all existing elements over.
* `getSize()` returns the current number of elements.
* `getCapacity()` returns the total capacity of the array.
constraints: At most `5000` method calls in total.

name: Max Consecutive Ones
description: Find the length of the longest contiguous subarray consisting only of the number `1` within a binary array `nums` containing only `0`s and `1`s.

name: Longest Common Prefix
description: Find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings `strs`. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
constraints: `strs[i]` consists of only lowercase English letters.

name: Replace Elements With Greatest Element On Right Side
description: Given an array `arr`, replace each element with the largest element among the elements to its right, and replace the last element with `-1`. Return the modified array.

name: Remove Element
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `val`, remove all occurrences of `val` in-place. The order of the elements may be changed. Return the number of elements in `nums` which are not equal to `val`.

name: Number of Students Unable to Eat Lunch
description: You are given two integer arrays `students` and `sandwiches` where `sandwiches[i]` is the type of the sandwich in the stack and `students[j]` is the preference of the student in the queue. Return the number of students unable to eat.

name: Majority Element
description: Given an array `nums` of size `n`, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than `⌊n / 2⌋` times. You may assume that the majority element always exists in the array.
